lesson.title: Simplifying Radicals
lesson.body: A radical is **simplified** when no perfect square factor remains under the radical sign. Use the **product rule**: √(ab) = √a · √b. Steps: (1) Factor out the largest perfect square from the radicand. (2) Take its square root outside. (3) Leave the rest inside. Example: √48 = √(16·3) = 4√3. Also: √(x⁴) = x² and √(x⁵) = x²√x. The **quotient rule**: √(a/b) = √a/√b. Always rationalize denominators: rewrite fractions so no radical is in the denominator.

rooms.question: Simplify: √72
rooms.explanation: Factor out the largest perfect square: 72 = 36 × 2. √72 = √36 · √2 = 6√2. Using 9×8 instead gives 3√8, which isn't fully simplified because √8 = 2√2. Always use the largest perfect square factor first.

rooms.question: Simplify: √(x⁶)
rooms.explanation: √(x⁶) = √((x³)²) = x³ (assuming x ≥ 0). General rule: √(xⁿ) = x^(n/2). For even exponents, divide by 2. For odd: √(x⁵) = x²√x (take out the largest even power).

rooms.question: Simplify: √(50x⁴y³)
rooms.explanation: Split: √50 = 5√2; √(x⁴) = x²; √(y³) = √(y²·y) = y√y. Combine: 5x²y√(2y). Check dimensions: 5²=25, x⁴, y²·(2y) = 2y³ → under radical: 25·x⁴·2y³ = 50x⁴y³ ✓.

rooms.question: Rationalize: 3/√5
rooms.explanation: Multiply numerator and denominator by √5: (3/√5)(√5/√5) = 3√5/5. This removes the radical from the denominator. The denominator becomes √5·√5 = 5. Standard form requires no radicals in the denominator.

rooms.question: Order the steps to simplify √(180):
items: Find the largest perfect square factor: 36 × 5 = 180
rooms.explanation: Factor → split → simplify the perfect square root → combine. Using 9×20 would give 3√20 = 3·2√5 = 6√5 — same answer, but takes an extra step. Choosing the largest perfect square factor first is always more efficient.

boss.name: The Radical Shade
boss.taunt: Simplify yourself out of my dungeon. You cannot.

questions.question: Which expression is NOT fully simplified?
questions.explanation: 2√12 is not simplified: √12 = √(4·3) = 2√3, so 2√12 = 2·2√3 = 4√3. The others — 5√3, 3√7, 4√5 — all have prime radicands (no perfect square factors), so they are fully simplified.
